title: hieracium pulchellum subsp. pulchellum
intro: Hieracium pulchellum subsp. pulchellum is a flowering plant species, commonly known as the small hawk's-beard or small hawkweed, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Hieracium pulchellum subsp. pulchellum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
